"  
Sec. 1  
[Consider the given equation and analyze the behavior of the function on both sides of the equation. The left-hand side of the equation is a combination of a quartic term and an absolute value term, which suggests that the function is even in nature. This means the function will have symmetry about the y-axis. The right-hand side of the equation is a constant, which implies that the number of solutions will depend on how many times the graph of the left-hand side intersects the horizontal line y = 7. Since the absolute value term ensures non-negative values, and the quartic term will dominate for large values of x, the function will eventually increase without bound as x moves away from zero. This means that there will be two points of intersection on the positive side of the x-axis and two points of intersection on the negative side of the x-axis, leading to a total of four real solutions.]  

Sec. 2  
[To determine the exact number of real solutions, consider the function f(x) = (1/3)x^4 + 5|x|. This function is continuous and differentiable everywhere except at x = 0, where the derivative of the absolute value term changes. However, since the absolute value term is non-negative and the quartic term is always non-negative, the function f(x) will always be greater than or equal to zero. As x approaches positive or negative infinity, the quartic term will dominate, causing f(x) to increase without bound. Therefore, the function will intersect the horizontal line y = 7 exactly twice on the positive side of the x-axis and twice on the negative side, resulting in four real solutions in total.]
"
